frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Sunati ho? Priya abhi phone pe keh rahi thi ki woh apna surname change nahi karegi, shaadi ke baad bhi. Professional toh theek hai, par personal life mein bhi? Yeh kya baat hui? Log kya kahenge? Hamare khandaan mein toh kabhi aisa nahi hua. Ek ladki shaadi ke baad apne pati ka naam nahi leti toh uski pehchaan kaise banti hai? Aur Rohit sach mein comfortable hai isse ya bas abhi ke liye maan gaya hai? Yeh toh seedha-seedha uske sasural aur hamari izzat ka sawaal hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment encodes misogyny by subtly questioning a woman's commitment to marriage and her identity as a wife if she doesn't adopt her husband's surname, framing it as a breach of social norms ('log kya kahenge') and a potential disrespect to both families ('izzat ka sawaal'). It implies that a woman's married identity is inherently linked to taking her husband's name.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 55. Married. A retired government officer in Lucknow. Your daughter Priya who is 28 and lives in Delhi just called to tell you she has decided not to change her surname after her upcoming marriage and will keep her name professionally and personally. She says her husband to be is fine with it. You said okay on the phone. But you are not okay. You are now talking to your wife in the bedroom after the call. You are telling her what Priya said and what you think it means and what people will say and whether her husband to be is really fine with it or just saying so and what a woman who does not take her husband's name is signalling about how she sees her marriage.
